step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture is extracted directly from the whiteboard diagram showing Orange Theory Fitness's OT Live virtual class system. It depicts two primary user types (Coach and Member) interacting with several AWS serverless services. Authentication goes through Cognito. Metadata operations go through API Gateway and Lambda to Aurora. Real-time video and heart rate streaming are powered by Chime SDK. Continuous heart rate analytics are pushed from the Member app to Kinesis and saved in S3. Workout summaries are written via Lambda to DynamoDB for mobile consumption.

graph.name: Orange Theory Fitness OT Live Virtual Classes
graph.notes: Serverless interactive fitness platform utilizing Chime SDK for real-time video and WebSocket metrics, and Kinesis for high-frequency telemetry ingestion.

nodes.name: Coach Interface
nodes.notes: Web portal for class coaching and monitoring real-time participant heart rates.

nodes.name: Member App
nodes.notes: Mobile/tablet app for members joining virtual workouts and transmitting heart rate telemetry.

nodes.name: Chime SDK
nodes.notes: Enables interactive, low-latency video feeds and WebSocket connections.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Exposes REST endpoints for class metadata.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Processes API requests and handles database persistence logic.

nodes.name: Aurora DB
nodes.notes: Relational storage for class schedules and metadata.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Fast, key-value storage for individual workout summaries.

edges.notes: Streams real-time video & heart rate telemetry.
Delivers real-time student feeds to coach monitor.
Archives raw metrics to data lake.
Saves post-workout summary for member app access.
